# ☕ Daily Tech Briefing
## [LLMs consistently pick resumes they generate over ones by humans or other models](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47932006)
* LLMs, including GPT-4, LLaMA 3.3, and Deepseek v3, exhibit a strong preference for resumes and executive summaries generated by themselves over those written by humans [https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47932006]
* This self-preferencing bias persists even when human annotators rate the human-written content as higher quality [https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47932006]
* A controlled resume experiment confirmed that LLMs consistently favor AI-generated resumes, suggesting a bias towards recognizing their own creations [https://marginalrevolution.com/marginalrevolution/2025/10/do-llms-favor-outputs-created-by-themselves.html]

## [Uber wants to turn its drivers into a sensor grid for AV companies](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/technology/articles/uber-wants-to-turn-its-millions-of-drivers-into-a-sensor-grid-for-self-driving-companies-063628164.html)
* Uber aims to leverage its extensive network of drivers to create a sensor grid for self-driving car companies [https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/technology/articles/uber-wants-to-turn-its-millions-of-drivers-into-a-sensor-grid-for-self-driving-companies-063628164.html]
* Uber's "Project Sentinel" utilizes driver smartphones and dashcams to gather real-time traffic and environmental data, creating a proprietary data lake [https://streamlinefeed.co.ke/news/uber-transforms-global-fleet-into-massive-autonomous-sensor-grid]

## [How fast is a macOS VM, and how small could it be?](https://eclecticlight.co/2024/02/06/summary-of-macos-vm-performance-on-apple-silicon-macs)
* macOS VMs on Apple silicon Macs offer performance close to the host, with single-core performance reaching 98% [Source Name](https://eclecticlight.co/2024/02/06/summary-of-macos-vm-performance-on-apple-silicon-macs)
* Disk image performance in macOS VMs can vary, with an unencrypted 100 GB APFS Disk Image showing a read speed of 7.8 GB/s and a write speed of 1.3 GB/s [Source Name](https://eclecticlight.co/2022/12/01/disk-performance-of-lightweight-macos-vms-on-apple-silicon)
* While not perfect, running macOS in a VM provides a suitable testing environment despite some performance overhead [Source Name](https://naumanahmad86.medium.com/how-little-ram-can-macos-really-run-on-94615327a921)
